items.title: Australian passengers on Hantavirus cruise ship land back home
items.standfirst: A group of Australian passengers who were aboard the Hantavirus-infected MV Hondius cruise ship have returned home.
items.content: A group of Australian passengers who were aboard the Hantavirus-infected MV Hondius cruise ship have returned home.

items.title: No vaccine, no cure: Here's what we know about hantavirus
items.standfirst: As Australian travellers linked to the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak land back in Western Australia, we look at what we do and don't know about the virus.
items.content: As Australian travellers linked to the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak land back in Western Australia, we look at what we do and don't know about the virus.

items.title: Passengers from hantavirus ship transferred to Australian quarantine
items.standfirst: A flight carrying five Australians and one New Zealander who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship has landed in an air force base in Perth.
items.content: A flight carrying five Australians and one New Zealander who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship has landed in an air force base in Perth.

items.title: Australian passengers on Hantavirus cruise ship en route home
items.standfirst: They have all tested negative for hantavirus and are being accompanied by medical professionals.
items.content: A group of Australian passengers who were aboard the Hantavirus-infected MV Hondius cruise ship are finally en route home.A repatriation aircraft carrying four Australian citizens, one permanent resident and one New Zealand citizen tonight departed the Netherlands for Australia.They have all tested negative for hantavirus and are being accompanied by medical professionals. READ MORE: Former Jacqui Lambie senator Tammy Tyrrell defects to LaborThe passengers were due to arrive at RAAF Base Pearce, about 35 kilometres north of Perth, about 11am (1pm AEST) on Friday.All flight passengers and crew aboard the charter flight will be dressed in full Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and will follow strict quarantine procedures during the flight and once they deboard.The group will then undergo a three-week quarantine at a facility at Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience.Health officials will then review the arrangement after the three-week period ends. The World Health Organisation (WHO) recommends a 42-day quarantine. The Bullsbrook centre was commissioned by the federal government in mid-2021, at the height of the COVID-19 pandemic.It was one of three such facilities designed to accommodate returning Australians, with a similar 500-bed centre built in Pinkenba, Brisbane, and a larger 1000-bed centre in Mickleham, Melbourne.Three of the Australian passengers are from NSW and two are from Queensland. The six passengers were initially told they could spend up to 48 hours transiting through the Netherlands under strict protocols before flying on to Perth.But authorities struggled to find a country willing to allow the charter plane to stop for refuelling on its way to Perth.Health Minister Mark Butler could not confirm where the plane would stop for fuel. He added that although the virus was "very, very rare" and human-to-human transmission was "rarer still", he was still taking a "precautionary approach".EXCLUSIVE: 'Can't sleep, can't eat': Families say they are blocked from NSW prisonsThere are now 11 confirmed cases of Hantavirus, all of which are from passengers or crew members on the MV Hondius.Three people have died after contracting the deadly, rat-borne illness.It was the first-ever case of a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, according to Maria Van Kerkhove, WHO's director of epidemic and pandemic preparedness.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

clusters.title: Man found dead in park kidnapped hours before death, police believe
clusters.neutral_headline: Man found dead in park kidnapped hours before death, police believe
bullet_summary: Police believe that a body found in a park in Melbourne's west is connected to a kidnapping hours before the discovery
A man's body was found near a children's playground in Melbourne's west after a violent home invasion in the city's north

items.title: Man found dead in park kidnapped hours before death, police believe
items.standfirst: Police believe that a body found in a park in Melbourne's west is connected to a kidnapping hours before the discovery.
items.content: Police believe that a body found in a park in Melbourne's west is connected to a kidnapping hours before the discovery.

items.title: Man found dead in park kidnapped hours before death, police believe
items.standfirst: "It appears at this point in time that the [victim] was somewhat targeted."
items.content: Police say that an armed home invasion in Melbourne's north-east is linked to the discovery of a body in a Melbourne park early this morning.Detective Inspector Dean Thomas said an aggravated home invasion occurred hours before the grisly discovery, which he believes is connected.Thomas said they have spoken to the family of the 30-year-old man, who was found near Bruni Drive in Tarneit at 4.30am, and a postmortem is set to determine the cause of death."It appears at this point in time that the [victim] was somewhat targeted." At about 3am, multiple men, allegedly armed with machetes, broke into a home on Darius Terrace in South Morang in a dark-coloured SUV.Police say they assaulted the man, who is known to police, while he was asleep, dragging him out of the house and into the vehicle before fleeing.READ MORE: Two boys arrested after alleged arson attack on Melbourne bar Another man and two children were inside the home at the time but were uninjured.Shortly after, emergency crews were called to a parkland about an hour away from the home after it was reported that a man was found dead."We believe that male is the deceased we have found this morning in Tarneit," Thomas said.Police are now appealing to the public for information and have yet to make any arrests, as investigations continue.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
